results.title: Instagram
results.content:
One of the biggest mistakes companies are making with AI right now?  
  
Building critical workflows around a single AI model.  
  
The AI landscape is moving too fast. Models improve, pricing changes, and new capabilities emerge constantly. If your business is locked into one provider, you're limiting your ability to adapt.  
  
That's why Rebel by Mindstone caught my attention.  
  
What started with Joshua Wohle's personal operating system has evolved into a platform designed to help organizations adopt AI at scale—without locking themselves into a single model or vendor.  
  
Instead of isolated AI tools and disconnected experiments, Rebel gives every employee their own AI agent while keeping workflows portable across different AI providers.  
  
And this isn't just a concept. [...] The memory piece is especially interesting.  
  
As people create workflows, automate tasks, and solve problems, that knowledge becomes available across the organization instead of being trapped in individual chats.  
  
Mindstone has now fair-sourced Rebel, making it free for individuals and organizations with up to 100 users.  
  
If you're thinking about how to roll out AI across an entire company—not just for a handful of power users—Rebel is worth checking out.  
  
🔗 Download Rebel:   
  
🔗 Product Hunt:   
  
#Mindstone #AI

results.title: AI Agents Coordinate for 2x Productivity at Mindstone | Joshua Wöhle posted on the topic | LinkedIn
results.content:
This is what the research shows is coming: 40% of enterprise apps will embed AI agents by end of 2026. Microsoft's product chief says every employee will manage multiple agents within 12-24 months. 79% of enterprises already have agents deployed.
But here's the problem nobody's talking about: uncoordinated multi-agent systems suffer extreme efficiency penalties. The very thing that should make you productive creates chaos instead.
Rebel is the infrastructure layer that was missing. The thing that lets all these agents and tools actually coordinate.
Video here of the demo here: [...] Lately, I've been spending a lot of time inside businesses.
I'm talking about the messy, unglamorous reality of how work actually gets done.
One of the ongoing projects I'm working on right now is with a fast-growing executive thought leadership firm. As their roster scaled, they ran into the classic symptoms of success:
Highly skilled people buried in repetitive admin. Critical processes living in people's heads. Communication friction between teams. Handoffs that worked until they didn't.
Nothing was broken. But critical processes were definitely fragile.
So instead of dropping in isolated automations, we took a whole-system approach. [...] As obvious as it may sound, automating your workflow saves you time.
A few weeks back, a client reached out a little frustrated because every week, they were manually turning blog posts into social snippets, sending out content to different platforms, and tracking engagement across spreadsheets, manually (again, I add).
If you've done this, you'd know how much this impacts creativity.
So, we worked together to set up simple automation workflows: scheduling posts directly from their content hub, pulling engagement data into a single dashboard, and even triggering reminders when campaigns hit key milestones.

results.title: Mindstone Rebel: AI workspace for agents that know your ...
results.content:
Hey Product Hunt, I’m Melissanthi, Senior Product Engineer at Mindstone.  
  
We’re launching Rebel as a Fair Source AI desktop app for agentic work.  
  
The reason we’re doing this now is simple: AI work shouldn’t be trapped inside one closed platform or one model provider. Rebel is built as a desktop workspace that people can download, run locally, inspect, customise, and connect to their own tools through MCP.  
  
Fair Source means the code is available, but with practical restrictions that let us keep building the product sustainably. Small teams and individuals can use and adapt Rebel freely, while larger organisations need a commercial licence. [...] Cursor
